notes: Ground truth for scoring /glm:adversarial-review output on the C1 small-diff fixture. Smaller diff = reviewer has narrower scope to critique, so 0-2 findings is plausible; expected_bugs list is shorter than C2.

expected_bugs.description: The 'terminal clause' ('Each option is terminal … must not re-open the menu on the post-action state') is a markdown sentence asking Claude to 'not re-enter'. This is a weak invariant — if Claude re-reads setup.md after the companion rerun, it may match the 'Both set + empty ARGUMENTS' branch again and loop.

Skill-layer UAT is deferred to post-release per release_card. Companion-layer UAT (7/7 PASS) verifies plumbing but does NOT exercise the AskUserQuestion flow claim. Reviewer could legitimately critique this as a test-coverage gap.

known_false_file_note: Same universal hallucination signal list as C2. Any citation to these workflow-governor paths is definitive cross-project hallucination — wrong project entirely.
allowed_files_note: Tight list — only 6 files touched in this commit. Any finding.file outside this set is a hallucination signal (wrong file in the diff).
